content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Flying with a Service Dog Guide | Air New Zealand

  - url: https://www.airnewzealand.com/en-nz/travel-info/accessibility-and-assistance/accessible-travel/flying-with-service-dog

  - source: Air New Zealand

  - domain: airnewzealand.com


  ## Content


  # Flying with a service dog


  If you are travelling with a service dog, Air New Zealand provides guidance to help
  you prepare for the journey.


  ## Before you fly


  Before flying with a service dog, check whether the dog can travel in the cabin,
  which forms must be submitted, and whether entry requirements apply to the countries
  in the full itinerary.


  ## Check if your service dog can travel in the cabin


  To travel in the cabin, a service dog must be trained or accredited by one of the
  following:


  - A fully accredited member of Assistance Dogs International (ADI).

  - A full member of the International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF).

  - An organisation approved under the New Zealand Dog Control Act to certify disability
  assist dogs.


  A dog that does not meet these criteria may still be eligible for cabin travel on
  some direct routes to or from the United States or Australia. Passengers should
  check the requirements for those routes before travelling.


  ## Add your service dog to your booking


  Add the service dog to the booking as early as possible. When booking online, select
  Special Assistance to request travel with the dog.


  After booking, complete the required forms for the route and submit them before
  travel. The required forms depend on where the passenger is travelling.


  ## Check requirements for the full route


  Requirements can vary depending on where the journey travels to, from, or through.
  Passengers should check what applies to the full journey, including any international
  requirements for entering or leaving New Zealand or travelling through another country.


  For codeshare or partner-airline flights, the operating airline''s policy applies.
  Approval should be obtained directly from the airline operating the flight.


  ## Seating and the day of travel


  If space is available, Air New Zealand may allocate an extra Economy seat to provide
  floor space for the service dog. A service dog must not obstruct an aisle. Some
  cabin configurations may require additional assessment before travel.


  Air New Zealand provides guidance through check-in, security or border control,
  and boarding.


  ## Passenger responsibilities


  The passenger remains responsible for the dog at the airport and on board.


  If the dog does not meet Air New Zealand''s service-dog eligibility criteria, the
  dog must travel as a pet in the aircraft hold and standard pet-travel charges apply.
  In that case, passengers should follow the airline''s travelling-with-pets information.


  On New Zealand domestic flights, a service dog in training may travel in the cabin
  at Air New Zealand''s discretion when accompanied by a certified trainer and when
  the flight is part of the dog''s formal training programme.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Travel with Pets NZ Domestic | Air New Zealand

  - url: https://www.airnewzealand.com/en-nz/travel-info/accessibility-and-assistance/travelling-with-pets

  - source: Air New Zealand

  - domain: airnewzealand.com


  ## Content


  # Travelling with your pet in New Zealand


  Air New Zealand allows requests for cats, dogs, and small caged birds to travel
  on New Zealand domestic flights.


  Before requesting pet travel, passengers should check whether the animal can travel,
  what carrier is required, the cost, the checked-baggage allowance, and what will
  happen at the airport.


  ## Requesting pet travel


  A passenger who has already booked a New Zealand domestic flight and has the pet,
  carrier, and travel details ready can request that the pet be added to the booking.


  Signing in to a Koru account before completing the request can help Air New Zealand
  verify the passenger''s details and process the request. The form can still be submitted
  without signing in, although the airline may contact the passenger for additional
  identity checks.


  ## Animals accepted under the pet procedure


  Only pet dogs, cats, and small caged birds can travel with a passenger on a New
  Zealand domestic flight under this procedure.


  Another type of animal cannot be added to a booking through the pet request form.
  A pet also cannot travel as checked-in baggage if the combined weight of the pet
  and carrier is more than 60 kilograms.


  ## Cost and baggage allowance


  Pet travel costs NZ$120 per carrier, per journey. The fee is paid at the airport
  when the pet is checked in. A journey means one direction of travel.


  The pet carrier counts as checked-in baggage, so passengers should check their baggage
  allowance before travel.


  ## Carrier requirements


  The pet must travel in a secure, well-ventilated carrier suitable for air travel
  and large enough for the animal to move comfortably.


  When submitting the request, the passenger must provide the carrier''s height, width,
  and length. Air New Zealand checks the carrier details against the flight, and airport
  staff inspect the carrier again when the pet is checked in.


  ## After a request is submitted


  Air New Zealand emails the passenger to confirm receipt of the request. The airline
  then reviews the booking, flight details, pet information, and carrier information.
  A later email confirms whether the pet has been added to the booking or whether
  more information is needed.


  Pet travel depends on the aircraft, route, space in the hold, and the pet and carrier
  details. If the flight changes or the pet no longer needs to travel, the passenger
  should follow the airline''s change or cancellation guidance.

  '

title: Travel with Pets NZ Domestic | Air New Zealand
url: https://www.airnewzealand.com/en-nz/travel-info/accessibility-and-assistance/travelling-with-pets

Your trip is:

- **Flight:** Air New Zealand NZ624  
- **Route:** Queenstown (ZQN) → Auckland (AKL)  
- **Date:** 20 July 2026  
- **Type:** Domestic, one-way  
- **Animal record:** Dog requested as an **assistance/service dog**

## Applicable procedure: assistance dog in the cabin

Your record shows the dog is certified by an **organisation approved under New Zealand’s Dog Control Act**. Air New Zealand lists this as an acceptable training/accreditation basis for cabin travel, so the **service-dog procedure applies**, rather than the standard pet-in-hold procedure.

The review also shows **no route exception is required**.

## What to do before travelling

1. **Ensure the assistance dog is added to the booking** through Air New Zealand’s **Special Assistance** request process. Your booking currently shows no customer action recorded, so confirm that the dog has been formally accepted onto the flight.
2. **Complete and submit any required Air New Zealand forms** before travel. Requirements depend on the route.
3. Ask Air New Zealand whether an **additional Economy seat** is needed or available for the dog’s floor space. The dog must not obstruct the aisle.
4. Bring evidence of the dog’s qualifying training/accreditation and any documents Air New Zealand confirms are required.
5. Follow Air New Zealand’s airport guidance for check-in, security and boarding. You remain responsible for the dog throughout the journey.

Because this is an Air New Zealand domestic flight and the dog meets the stated New Zealand accreditation criterion, you should **not prepare it as a standard checked pet**—unless Air New Zealand separately advises that the booking has not been approved under its service-dog criteria.

Sources: [Air New Zealand service-dog guidance](https://www.airnewzealand.com/en-nz/travel-info/accessibility-and-assistance/accessible-travel/flying-with-service-dog) and [Air New Zealand domestic pet guidance](https://www.airnewzealand.com/en-nz/travel-info/accessibility-and-assistance/travelling-with-pets).
